title: festuca rubra subsp. mediana
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. mediana, commonly known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Festuca rubra subsp. mediana have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system health. Festuca rubra subsp. mediana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in the form of essential oils for topical applications.
